cases.prompt: set up linting for this typescript project
should we use biome or eslint?
migrate our eslintrc to flat config
add prettier and make it play nice with eslint
our linter is slow, can we switch to something faster
configure biome for this repo
lint + format setup pls
eslint keeps fighting prettier, fix the config
tune the lint rules, too many false positives
add import sorting and unused-import removal
tighten the tsconfig strictness
set up vitest for this package
add a CI matrix for node versions
fix the failing type check
configure package.json exports
publish this package to npm
harden next.config for production
audit our dependencies for malicious packages
set up linting and formatting for a new TypeScript library
expected_behavior: asks about or detects plugin needs before choosing: Biome as the one-tool default, ESLint v10 flat config + Prettier when the plugin ecosystem is needed
writes flat config only for ESLint (eslintrc is removed in v10) and never mixes formatters
wires format-check and lint into package.json scripts and points CI at them
runs the skill's verifier script to confirm the config parses and the tools run clean

cases.prompt: migrate this eslintrc + prettier setup to something faster
expected_behavior: offers the two real paths: ESLint v10 flat-config migration or a Biome migration (biome migrate eslint), with honest plugin-coverage tradeoffs
preserves intentional rule customizations instead of resetting to defaults
verifies the migrated setup produces no new errors on the existing codebase before deleting the old config

cases.prompt: biome for a next.js app - good idea?
expected_behavior: explains eslint-config-next coverage (Next-specific and RSC rules) that Biome does not fully replace
recommends keeping eslint-config-next for Next apps or a split setup (Biome format + ESLint next rules) with the tradeoff stated
does not claim Biome replaces tsc type checking
